ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers raw tomatoes; green (unripe) variant is a sub-type within this category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT code 734881000 refers to tomato as a substance and is the closest parent concept; no distinct green tomato or unripe tomato code identified in SNOMED CT.

serving.natural_language_portion: One medium raw green tomato weighs approximately 123 g and provides around 28 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Tomatoes, green, raw — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 170457)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for raw green tomatoes used as primary nutrient reference. HPB Singapore values (energy 23 kcal, protein 1.2 g, fat 0.2 g, carbohydrate 5.1 g, sugar 4.0 g, sodium 13 mg, dietary fibre 1.1 g, potassium 204 mg, calcium 13 mg, iron 0.51 mg, cholesterol 0 mg) are consistent with USDA SR Legacy data and are retained. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and micronutrient values supplemented from USDA SR Legacy where HPB did not report them.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Tomatoes — University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate based on ripe tomato GI (15, low GI) applied to green tomato
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for raw green tomato found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 15 is adopted from the published GI for ripe raw tomato (GI ~15, low) as the closest defensible analogous food. Green tomatoes have lower sugar content than ripe tomatoes, so GI 15 is a conservative upper-bound estimate. GL per serving calculated as: (15 × 5.1 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 123 g serving) / 100 = 0.94, rounded to 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (13 mg/100 g) and meaningful potassium (204 mg/100 g) supporting a favourable Na:K ratio
hypertension.guidance: Raw green tomato is appropriate for hypertensive individuals; its very low sodium content and potassium level align with DASH dietary principles for blood pressure management.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (5.1 g/100 g), low estimated GI (~15), and dietary fibre (1.1 g/100 g) supporting modest postprandial glucose modulation
type2_diabetes.guidance: Raw green tomato is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; its low carbohydrate density, low glycaemic index, and fibre content contribute to attenuated postprandial blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low saturated fat (0.03 g/100 g), zero cholesterol, and potassium content supporting cardiovascular health
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Raw green tomato is cardioprotective-friendly; negligible saturated fat, zero cholesterol, and potassium content support vascular health, and its vitamin C and antioxidant profile may contribute to endothelial function.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Nightshade vegetable containing solanine alkaloids that may exacerbate joint inflammation in susceptible individuals
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Green tomato is a nightshade and its solanine content may trigger or worsen joint inflammation in susceptible rheumatoid arthritis patients; individual tolerance should be monitored and consumption avoided during flares.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Raw green tomato (Solanum lycopersicum) is the unripe form of the common tomato, harvested before full ripening. It has a firm, crisp texture and a distinctly tart, slightly acidic flavour profile compared to the sweeter ripe red tomato. Green tomatoes are used raw in salads, pickled preparations, chutneys, and are famously fried in Southern American cuisine. Nutritionally, raw green tomato is a low-calorie vegetable providing approximately 23 kcal per 100 g, with low carbohydrate (5.1 g), modest protein (1.2 g), and negligible fat (0.2 g). It provides dietary fibre (1.1 g), vitamin C (23.4 mg), potassium (204 mg), and folate (14 mcg) per 100 g. Sodium content is very low at 13 mg/100 g. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 15 (low), making it suitable for blood glucose management. As a nightshade vegetable, it contains solanine alkaloids and is generally cautioned for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis or nightshade sensitivity. It is naturally cholesterol-free, vegan, gluten-free, and halal-suitable.
common_user_queries: Is green tomato good for diabetes?
Can I eat green tomato if I have high blood pressure?
What is the calorie count of a green tomato?
Green tomato vs red tomato nutrition
Can rheumatoid arthritis patients eat green tomatoes?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced from HPB Singapore database (confidence 0.95) and cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for raw green tomatoes (FDC ID 170457). Values are consistent across both approved sources. Micronutrient values supplemented from USDA SR Legacy. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using ripe tomato as the closest approved reference.
notes: Cholesterol is confirmed zero (0.0 mg/100 g) per HPB Singapore and USDA SR Legacy — green tomatoes are plant-based foods that naturally contain no cholesterol. Vitamin B12 and vitamin D are 0.0 as green tomatoes are plant-based and do not naturally contain these nutrients. Trans fat is 0.0 as green tomatoes contain no industrially produced trans fats. Saturated fat (0.03 g), monounsaturated fat (0.03 g), polyunsaturated fat (0.08 g), and micronutrient values (vitamin C, vitamin A, B-vitamins, folate, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, water) are sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 170457) as HPB did not report these fields. GI of 15 is an analogous estimate based on ripe raw tomato GI from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for raw green tomato was found in approved sources. GL per serving = (15 × 5.1 × 1.23) / 100 = 0.94, rounded to 0.9. This food is a nightshade vegetable; solanine content is a relevant consideration for rheumatoid arthritis patients. SNOMED CT match type set to 'closest_parent' as code 734881000 (Tomato, substance) is the nearest parent concept; no specific green tomato code exists in SNOMED CT.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 3: Corrected ontology.snomed_ct_match_type from 'broader' to 'closest_parent', which is one of the three allowed values (exact, closest_parent, none) per the RESULT template and validator feedback. Updated snomed_ct_gap_note to reflect the closest_parent match type. Updated notes field to document the SNOMED CT match type correction. All other sections preserved from iteration 2 as they passed validation.
